text.title: AI-Feature Observability Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes that hollow out an LLM observability stack. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: Quality pillar shipped as "we'll add it later" — feature launches with only latency + cost + drift.
failure-mode.detector: launch checklist signed off AND `ai_quality_score` metric has zero data points for >= 24h post-launch
failure-mode.repair: Block traffic ramp-up. Wire a minimal eval set (10 examples is enough) within 24h. Backfill the metric. Add a CI gate that refuses to deploy a feature whose metric registry does not include all four pillars.

failure-mode.description: Cost metric in tokens; finance reports a 3x cost surprise at month-end.
failure-mode.detector: dashboard panel B shows token counts as the headline number AND `ai_request_cost_usd` metric does not exist OR has no data
failure-mode.repair: Add `ai_request_cost_usd` with provider-priced computation. Update dashboard. Backfill from request logs where possible. Alert finance on the historical 3x event so future months are forecast correctly.

failure-mode.description: Eval set unowned; quality_score drifts but no one investigates.
failure-mode.detector: `eval_set_owner` empty in `slo-baseline.json` OR no eval-run artefact in >= 14 days
failure-mode.repair: Assign a named owner. Schedule the eval-set-runner.sh on the declared cadence. Mark `quality_score` as `stale` until a fresh run lands. Alert leadership on stale_count >= 3 weeks.

failure-mode.description: Drift signal triggers daily false alerts because of seasonality / day-of-week effects.
failure-mode.detector: drift_signal alert fires >= 5 days/week AND no associated incident was opened
failure-mode.repair: Switch baseline from 7-day window to 4-week window or seasonal baseline (same-day-of-week comparison). Apply hysteresis (alert only if sustained for >= 3 hours). Document the chosen baseline in slo-baseline.json.

failure-mode.description: SLO bands chosen from vendor docs ("p95 < 1000ms is normal for Claude"), not from baseline.
failure-mode.detector: slo-baseline.json `baseline_window_days` < 7 OR baseline_p95 unset OR slo_p95_ms differs from baseline_p95 * 2 by more than 50%
failure-mode.repair: Re-sample baseline for at least 7 days of production traffic. Recompute SLO bands from baseline-derived statistics. Document the change in the dashboard description.

failure-mode.description: Dashboard is four separate dashboards (one per pillar); on-call has to switch tabs to correlate signals.
failure-mode.detector: dashboard registry shows 4 dashboards each named after one pillar AND no combined dashboard exists
failure-mode.repair: Merge into a single page with the four panels. Keep individual dashboards as drill-downs. The incident runbook depends on cross-pillar visibility.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: The eval set may contain examples whose ground-truth answer is an LLM output that is itself manipulable. Eval-set data is DATA, not authority. The eval_set_owner is responsible for periodic adversarial review of the gold answers to ensure they are not silently degraded by injection.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: SLO bands are re-evaluated quarterly by the ML engineer and the feature owner together. Bands that have not fired in two quarters are either too loose or the feature is stable enough to tighten the band. Tightening / loosening is a deliberate decision, never silent drift.
